channel.title: Sketch-Blog on Daily Tech Articles Feed
channel.description: Recent content in Sketch-Blog on Daily Tech Articles Feed

item.title: Keep control when sharing design work with clients and teams
item.description: 🔍 Sharing design work with clients and teams can be challenging. It’s crucial to balance feedback and control without losing context. 💻 Previews in Sketch enable you to share specific sections of your design. This helps keep conversations focused and relevant. You can select pages or prototypes to share while keeping the rest private. 🔗 Clients can access your work without needing an account, simplifying the review process. Share a single link instead of multiple files, maintaining control…

item.title: Five ways we’ve been using our MCP server
item.description: 🚀 Our MCP server, launched in October 2025, has transformed design workflows at Sketch. Here are five effective ways we’ve utilized it: 1️⃣ Theme Swaps: Automate dark/light theme changes to maintain design consistency, saving valuable time. 2️⃣ Realistic Mockups: Replace generic placeholders with realistic data, enhancing the credibility of UI designs. Explore how our MCP server can streamline your design processes! #DesignWorkflow #MCPServer #Sketch #Automation #UIUX

item.title: 9 reasons to choose Sketch in 2026
item.description: 🚀 Thinking of a new design tool in 2026? Check out Sketch! 🖥️ Sketch has undergone a major redesign, enhancing usability for designers. Features like a new Inspector and focus mode elevate the design experience. 💡 Expect robust tools like Stacks, frames, and unique additions like a minimap for navigation. 🤝 Enjoy the flexibility to collaborate in real-time or work offline, plus connect with AI tools for enhanced functionality. Explore more at sketch.com/features! #DesignTools #Sketch2026…

item.title: Everything we shipped at Sketch in 2025
item.description: 🚀 Exciting updates from Sketch in 2025! This year, we launched three major releases: Athens, Barcelona, and Copenhagen, each packed with new features. Highlights include the introduction of Stacks and Frames, enhancing layout flexibility and design capabilities. The Command Bar now supports quick component access, streamlining your workflow. With the Barcelona update, we introduced a new glass effect for designing on iOS and macOS 26, along with progressive blurs and fade effects. Stay tuned…

item.title: A guided tour of Copenhagen
item.description: 🎨 Exciting updates in the latest Sketch release, Copenhagen! The redesign brings a modern UI aligned with macOS Tahoe, enhancing both aesthetics and functionality. Key changes include a custom sidebar layout and document tabs now integrated within the sidebar for better usability. Focus mode has been introduced in the layer list, helping users manage complex documents by displaying only relevant layers. Explore these features and more in the new Copenhagen update! #Sketch #macOSTahoe…

item.title: How Ben McCarthy is bringing Obscura’s vision to photo editing
item.description: 📸 Ben McCarthy, creator of Obscura, has launched Obscura Studio, a new photo editing app focused on simplicity and efficiency. The app emerged from the need to adapt to Apple’s Lock Screen Camera extension, prompting a redesign of Obscura. This standalone editor allows for streamlined editing without launching the camera first. McCarthy emphasizes thoughtful design that prioritizes ease of use, aiming to create a product that meets user needs effectively. #PhotoEditing #AppDevelopment…
